acceptance_targets: [CRAFT_TELEPATHIC_HANDOUT] Pass if a cold reader can distinguish what Bretz actually thinks on the page from what Anya infers, and if snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:275 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:279 no longer present the full Hartig chain with certainty unless each claim has explicit on-page support. Fail if the revised scene still delivers the reveal as one clean mind-dump.

[CRAFT_OVERSTATEMENT_AFTER_HARTIG] Pass if the revised material around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:233 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:249 contains only one clear rendering of Bretz's resignation and the later beat adds new action or pressure. Fail if both locations still restate the same abstract diagnosis of Bretz's conscience.
[RED-028] Ch 5 em-dash-containing prose lines are at or below 8%.

[LW02-002] By the time chapter 05 reaches the club approach, the reader should know why Damian is willing to put Anya beside him in a couples cover even though chapter 04 ended on provisional trust rather than earned trust.

[LW02-003] A reader moving from chapter 03 to chapter 05 should be able to describe two distinct recognition beats with different mechanics or different consequences, not 'Damian almost remembers because of her laugh' twice.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a cold reader can distinguish what Bretz actually thinks on the page from what Anya infers, and if snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:275 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:279 no longer present the full Hartig chain with certainty unless each claim has explicit on-page support. Fail if the revised scene still delivers the reveal as one clean mind-dump.
findings.problem: The chapter's central intelligence turn arrives as a telepathic handout. Bretz's mind supplies the exact name, office, amount, duration, routing method, and implied higher sponsor in a clean cascade, so Anya receives a dossier-grade answer instead of fighting through the fragmentary surface-thought limits established for her ability. That makes the Hartig reveal read as convenience rather than extraction.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rebuild the reveal around partial, immediately active worries at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:193; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:199; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:227; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:231, then make Anya assemble the pattern in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:275 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:279. Bretz can think 'Hartig,' 'Tuesday,' 'four hundred thousand,' 'secondary channel,' 'discretionary fund,' but the chapter should force Anya to infer the theory from fragments plus an external cue in scene instead of receiving the whole procurement explanation in one internal briefing.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised material around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:233 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:249 contains only one clear rendering of Bretz's resignation and the later beat adds new action or pressure. Fail if both locations still restate the same abstract diagnosis of Bretz's conscience.
findings.problem: After the chapter has already landed both the Hartig lead and Bretz's exhausted compliance, it translates the same point twice in abstract diagnosis. The repeated 'he wasn't corrupt / he was tired / he chose compliance' explanation slows the exit and turns a live scene into commentary.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:233; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:235; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:249; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:251 so Bretz's compromised resignation is rendered once through a concrete thought, gesture, or spoken dodge, then move the scene forward into Damian's return. If you keep the moral read, anchor it to something Bretz does under pressure instead of two narrator verdicts.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 5 em-dash-containing prose lines are at or below 8%.
findings.evidence: Ch 5: 72 of 191 prose lines (37.7%) contain em-dashes.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reduce em-dash-containing lines in Ch 5 from 72 to ~15 (target ~8%).

findings.acceptance_test: By the time chapter 05 reaches the club approach, the reader should know why Damian is willing to put Anya beside him in a couples cover even though chapter 04 ended on provisional trust rather than earned trust.
findings.evidence: Chapter 04 ends with Damian recognizing that Anya's explanation for Brandt is false and explicitly downgrading the alliance to something workable but untrusted at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1034, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1036, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1040, and snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1044. Chapter 05 then opens with them already sharing club intelligence in Damian's car and defaulting to an intimacy-heavy couples cover for their first joint operation at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1077, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1095, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1097, and snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1105.

evidence.excerpt: 1034: "My service has been monitoring Renewal Front activity channels," she said, which was true in the broadest sense and false in every specific one. "Brandt's name came up. When I saw him working you at the forum, I recognized the approach."

1036: Damian held her gaze for a long moment. His thoughts: *She's lying. Not about all of it — the operational picture is probably real. But the specifics don't add up. She reacted too fast, too precisely. She didn't recognize an approach pattern. She knew, in real time, what he was about to do. That's not analysis. That's something else.*
1040: "Okay," he said. Not *I believe you.* Just *okay.* The word sat in the fluorescent corridor like a provisional thing, a handshake that both parties knew was temporary.

1044: *...she's intelligence. She's good. She just saved my operation, maybe my life if Gessler had escalated. And I don't trust her. But I don't have to trust her. I just have to work with her. And working with someone is not the same as trusting them, and the fact that I want to trust her is exactly the reason I shouldn't.*

1077: Anya focused on the Gesellschaft Club's membership roster instead. Damian had pulled it from somewhere — she didn't ask where, because their terms included not questioning each other's networks, and the less she knew about how a twenty-year-old diplomat obtained a private club's internal documents, the cleaner her operational conscience stayed. Not that her operational conscience was in great shape these days.
1095: "I still think we should approach him separately," Anya said. "You work the room, I find Bretz, we converge when I've got a read on his—"

1097: "No. Bretz is old guard. He respects couples. He respects men who bring their partners to social functions because it means they've got nothing to hide." Damian's voice had the measured quality of someone who'd thought this through several times. "If you approach him alone, you're a young woman asking questions. If we approach together, we're a couple making small talk."
1105: "We're — they'll expect us to be. Together."

findings.problem: The payoff chapter arrives one bridge beat too late. The scene itself carries tension, but the window skips the connective tissue between a fluorescent-corridor truce and a high-exposure undercover couple play. Damian has just filed Anya as a liar with an unknown method; the next chapter asks the reader to accept, before the chapter has justified it, that she is in his car, holding his dossier, and is the obvious partner for a boyfriend-girlfriend cover.
findings.rewrite_direction:
Add one concrete handoff beat at the end of chapter 04 or the top of chapter 05 that makes this escalation feel necessary rather than assumed. Show why this lead cannot wait, why Damian cannot run it through his own network first, and why couples cover with Anya is the least bad option despite his unresolved doubts.

Aggregator guidance:
- Cross-chapter context: Ch 4 ends with Damian having filed Anya as a liar whose specifics don't add up. He accepted the alliance out of operational necessity but has unresolved doubts about her identity and methods. Ch 5 needs to show why Damian puts Anya beside him for the undercover couple operation despite this provisional trust — the lead is too time-sensitive for his own network, the couples cover specifically requires a partner, or both.

findings.acceptance_test: A reader moving from chapter 03 to chapter 05 should be able to describe two distinct recognition beats with different mechanics or different consequences, not 'Damian almost remembers because of her laugh' twice.
findings.evidence: In chapter 03, Anya's real laugh makes Damian reach toward a school-memory recognition that does not quite land at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:731, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:733, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:735, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:781, and snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:783. In chapter 05, her laugh on the bench triggers the same basic circuit again: childhood image surfaces, the memory nearly resolves, then drops back under before anything changes in the spoken scene at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1401, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1407, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1409, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1413, and snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1455.

evidence.excerpt: 731: The thought was louder than anything she'd read from him all night. Not analytical. Not self-correcting. A raw, unfiltered sensory response — the sound of her laughter hitting something deep and unexamined in him, a memory he couldn't locate, a recognition that was almost but not quite—

733: *A girl at school. She laughed like that. Exactly like that. In a courtyard — no, a hallway — no, outside somewhere. After something happened. Something funny. She was laughing and I was standing there and I thought—*

735: The memory reached for completion. Anya could feel it stretching, trying to close the circuit — pink hair, a courtyard, a specific afternoon — and she watched it not quite get there. The sediment of a childhood stored away and rarely disturbed shifted but didn't fully surface. He blinked. Twice. His hands went into his pockets — the stress tell, the one that meant he'd felt something he couldn't categorize.
781: *The laugh. Something about her laugh. And the way she tilted her head when she listened. I've — someone — a specific girl. Pink hair. At a school. She used to—*

1401: *A schoolyard. The big linden tree near the east wall of the campus — the one everybody climbed, the one with the flat branch halfway up where you could sit and see the whole field. An afternoon. Autumn, like this. A girl sitting on that branch. She was — six? Seven? She'd beaten him at something. The relay race. The one where the teams were picked by the teachers and she ended up on the opposing side and she was FAST, impossibly fast for someone that small, and he'd been so sure he was going to win because he'd been training all week in the garden and—*
1407: *Pink,* Damian thought. *Her hair was pink. And she laughed just like—*

1409: He blinked. The memory wavered at the surface, almost whole. Anya could see it in his mind like watching someone reach for a glass on the edge of a table — the fingers stretching, brushing the rim. The name was close. Closer than it had ever been. A girl. A specific girl. Anya—
1413: *That's strange,* he thought. He shook his head — a tiny movement, barely visible. *That was a long time ago. Why did I — that girl. What was her—*

1455: She pulled her shoes back on, grimacing at the cold leather. They walked to the car without talking, their breath making brief clouds that dissolved in the lamplight, and Anya carried the name of a man who was moving money for people who wanted a war, and the sound of her own laugh living inside someone else's memory of a golden afternoon, and the knowledge that the boy walking beside her was one clear moment away from remembering everything.

findings.problem: Read as a cluster, chapter 05 reuses a beat chapter 03 has already spent significant capital on. The trigger is the same, Damian's internal motion is the same, and the result is functionally the same: private almost-recognition, no outward consequence, Anya silently noting that the fuse is shorter. Chapter 05 gives the reader richer detail, but not different leverage, so the escalation reads like a replay instead of a turn.
findings.rewrite_direction: Differentiate the second recognition beat by changing either the stimulus or the consequence. Let a new cue trip the memory, or let the same laugh force Damian into a spoken question, visible misstep, or tactical decision that materially changes the scene rather than staying inside his head.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel's premise blends comedy, espionage action, and genuine emotional stakes. The creative risk is in honoring all three simultaneously without letting any one mode cannibalize the others. Do not soften the violence because the novel is funny. Do not suppress the comedy because the stakes are real. Do not retreat from Anya's moral complicity (she reads minds without consent and uses it to manipulate) because she is the protagonist and we like her. The hardest scenes to get right are the ones where comedy and pain coexist — a fight that is also funny, a confession that is also absurd, a family dinner where love and deception are the same meal. Lean into those moments rather than resolving them into one register. The novel's best material lives in the dissonance.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism at the point of consequence is a craft failure. When violence, fear, grief, or moral compromise demands scene presence, render it with specificity. A bruise should have a color. A death should have weight. A lie should have a cost the liar feels in their body. The exception is Anya's telepathic restraint — she sometimes cannot bear to fully articulate what she's reading in someone's mind, and this selective omission is a character choice, not authorial squeamishness.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters in casual speech. Uncontracted forms reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or character-specific register choices (Sylvia's professional diction, Damian's diplomatic mode). In contemporary casual scenes, an unjustified full-form line should be flagged and checked.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: No two consecutive chapters should share the same structural rhythm. Alternate between dialogue-heavy scenes and interiority-heavy passages. Vary scene count: two-scene chapters (11, 16, 17 — compressed force) against multi-scene chapters (8, 15 — breadth and momentum). Shift between comic dominant and dramatic dominant across the middle of the book, with the comic register maintaining presence even in dramatic chapters through Anya's voice and the Forger family's inherent absurdity. The tonal shift at Chapter 13 is a deliberate gear change, not a gradual dimming — comedy should be fully alive in Chapter 12 and markedly reduced (not absent) in Chapter 13. The Chapter 13 POV shift to Damian is a structural event: the reader's relationship to information changes. The final act (14-17) maintains Anya's voice and the family's warmth as comic undertone, but the dominant mode is drama with consequences. Chapter 17's dinner scene reintroduces comedy through a register the novel has not previously accessed — Damian's self-conscious thought management around a known telepath — which only works because it follows sixteen chapters of oblivious thought-reading.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary and accessible, not literary-precious. The prose should have energy and forward drive. Humor comes from observation, character logic, and the gap between surface and interior — not from clever narration or authorial commentary. Let the funny things be funny without signposting them. Let the sad things sit without interpretation. The prose should be invisible when it needs to be and vivid when the scene demands it, but never decorative for its own sake.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Narrator-as-comedian: the prose commenting on its own comedy, winking at the reader, or admiring a character's wit. The humor lives in the situation, not in the telling.

Telepathy-as-omniscience: Anya's mind-reading should feel like cognitive overload, not like a narrator's convenient access to exposition. She reads surface thoughts, not deep motivations, and the thoughts she catches should be fragmentary, mundane, and sometimes misleading.

Prestige-creep: the prose drifting toward solemn literary register when the scene's natural mode is comic, warm, or chaotic. This novel's emotional power comes from the coexistence of comedy and genuine feeling, not from the comedy yielding to seriousness.

Briefing-itis: scenes where characters explain plot to each other or to the reader. Information should emerge through action, observation, and telepathic fragments — not through expository dialogue formatted as conversation.

Combat-poetry: fight scenes that become lyrical or aesthetically admiring. Violence should be fast, specific, physically grounded, and over quickly. Yor's lethality is matter-of-fact; Anya's fighting is scrappier and less elegant.

Emotion-labeling: telling the reader what a character feels instead of rendering the feeling through thought, action, and physical detail. 'She felt sad' is always worse than the specific thing that makes the sadness legible.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, primarily Anya. Chapter 7 (Damian), Chapter 10 (Yor), the Damian POV section in Chapter 13, and Damian's thread in Chapter 15 shift to close third on their respective POV characters. The telepathy layer is unique to Anya's POV — when she is the viewpoint character, other characters' surface thoughts appear as italicized interjections woven into the prose. In non-Anya POV chapters, no telepathic content appears. The Chapter 13 Damian POV section is structurally critical: it gives the reader access to Damian's interior at a depth Anya's telepathy cannot reach, and it reveals that he has identified Anya — knowledge the reader carries into the final act while Anya does not. This dramatic irony reversal is the novel's strongest structural surprise and depends on the reader experiencing Damian's depths unmediated by Anya's telepathic filter.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied and responsive to scene pressure. Action sequences: short sentences, concrete verbs, sensory immediacy. Comic scenes: longer sentences that build to turns, pacing that allows beats to land. Quiet domestic scenes: relaxed rhythm with room for observation and texture. Telepathic interjections should disrupt prose rhythm the way intrusive thoughts disrupt concentration — not ornamentally but as genuine cognitive interference. Avoid metronomic sentence patterns in any mode.
sensory_bias: visual surveillance — reading rooms, reading faces, the spy's hyperawareness of spatial arrangement and exit routes
auditory — overheard thoughts as a telepathic sixth sense layered over actual ambient sound
kinesthetic — action choreography, physical proximity, the body under stress or in intimacy
olfactory/gustatory — Yor's cooking, Berlint street food, the specific smell of institutional buildings and safe houses
